title: urochilus sanguineus
intro: Urochilus sanguineus, also known as the bleeding heart vine or coral bell, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical Asia, characterized by its vibrant red and yellow flowers. The medicinal properties of Urochilus sanguineus include its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Urochilus sanguineus can be prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
